data.title: Time Loop Facility Escape
data.statement:
Navigate through a facility to reach the extraction point {{E}} by coordinating robot actions across multiple time loops.

<<The Facility>>

A [[width]] × [[height]] grid containing the following symbols:

• {{S}}     <<Start>>:    All robots spawn here at the beginning of each loop
• {{E}}     <<Exit>>:     Reach this point to complete the mission
• {{#}}     <<Wall>>:     Impassable obstacle
• {{.}}     <<Floor>>:    Walkable space
• {{a-z}}   <<Switches>>: Pressure plates that control corresponding doors ({{e}} and {{s}} excluded)
• {{A-Z}}   <<Doors>>:    Barriers opened by the matching lowercase switch ({{E}} and {{S}} excluded). e.g., {{a}} opens {{A}}. 

<<Key Concepts>>

- <<Robot>>: The entity you control. Each loop, you program a robot's path.
- <<Ghost>>: A replay of a previous robot. After completing a loop, that robot becomes a ghost, which automatically replays the same sequence of moves in all subsequent loops. Ghosts remain stationary at their final position once their path ends.

<<Rules>>

<<1. Movement>>
Each turn, a robot may perform one of the following actions:
- {{D}} (<<Down>>): Move one cell down 
- {{L}} (<<Left>>): Move one cell left 
- {{R}} (<<Right>>): Move one cell right
- {{U}} (<<Up>>): Move one cell up 
- {{W}} (<<Wait>>): Stay in place

A robot cannot move into a wall ({{#}}) or a closed door ({{A-Z}}).

<<2. Time Loops>>
- You program paths sequentially: Loop {{0}}, then Loop {{1}}, etc.
- In Loop [[N]], ghosts from all previous loops ({{0}} to [[N]]-{{1}}) replay their paths simultaneously.
- The robot and all ghosts move together, one step per turn.

<<3. Collision>>
- A robot <<cannot>> occupy the same position as a ghost at the same time step.
- Robots and ghosts <<can>> cross each other (swap places) between time steps.
- {{S}} (start) is a neutral zone where collisions are ignored.

<<4. Door Activation>>
A door is open for the <<entire loop>> if any ghost will stand on its corresponding switch at any point during that loop. This follows time loop logic: future actions are predetermined. A robot cannot open a door for its own loop; only ghosts from previous loops can.

<<5. Victory Condition>>
The mission is complete when any robot reaches {{E}}.

<<6. Turn Counting>>
All loops run simultaneously, so the <<number of turns>> of a solution is defined as the length of a single <<padded>> output line. Padding with {{W}} counts as actual turns. {{W}} moves after reaching {{E}} still count towards the <<total number of turns>>.

<<7. Determining the Canonical Solution>>
If multiple solutions exist, the <<Canonical Solution>> is determined by applying the following criteria in order of priority:

<<7.1. Minimize Turns>>: Complete the mission in the fewest number of <<turns>> per loop. For example, a solution finishing in 7 turns per loop (with 2 loops) is preferred over one finishing in 9 turns (with 1 loop).

<<7.2. Minimize Loops>>: If the number of turns are equal, prefer the solution requiring fewer <<time loops>> (fewer output lines). For example, a solution with 2 loops (7 turns) is preferred over one with 3 loops (7 turns).

<<7.3. Lexicographical Order>>: If both the <<number of turns>> and the <<number of loops>> are equal, return the solution that is <<lexicographically largest>>. To compare two solutions, concatenate all their output lines into a single string (Loop 0, then Loop 1, etc.) and compare using standard string comparison. Since {{W}} has the highest ASCII value among the move characters, this ensures that robots prefer <<Waiting>> over performing unnecessary moves.

data.stubGenerator:
read width:int height:int maxLoops:int
loop height
    read row:string(width)
write "U R W..."
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1:>> Three space-separated integers [[width]] [[height]] [[max_loops]] 

<<Next [[height]] lines>>: The facility map (each row is [[width]] characters)
data.outputDescription:
<<[[N]] lines>> (where [[N]] ≤ [[max_loops]]), one per loop.

Each line contains space-separated single-letter moves: {{D}} {{L}} {{R}} {{U}} {{W}} (Down, Left, Right, Up, Wait).
<<Important>>: All paths must be padded to the same length using {{W}}.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Navigate through a facility to reach the extraction point E by coordinating robot actions across multiple time loops.

The Facility

A width × height grid containing the following symbols:

• S Start: All robots spawn here at the beginning of each loop
• E Exit: Reach this point to complete the mission
• # Wall: Impassable obstacle
• . Floor: Walkable space
• a-z Switches: Pressure plates that control corresponding doors (e and s excluded)
• A-Z Doors: Barriers opened by the matching lowercase switch (E and S excluded). e.g., a opens A.

Key Concepts

- Robot: The entity you control. Each loop, you program a robot's path.
- Ghost: A replay of a previous robot. After completing a loop, that robot becomes a ghost, which automatically replays the same sequence of moves in all subsequent loops. Ghosts remain stationary at their final position once their path ends.

Rules

1. Movement
Each turn, a robot may perform one of the following actions:
- D (Down): Move one cell down
- L (Left): Move one cell left
- R (Right): Move one cell right
- U (Up): Move one cell up
- W (Wait): Stay in place

A robot cannot move into a wall (#) or a closed door (A-Z).

2. Time Loops
- You program paths sequentially: Loop 0, then Loop 1, etc.
- In Loop N, ghosts from all previous loops (0 to N-1) replay their paths simultaneously.
- The robot and all ghosts move together, one step per turn.

3. Collision
- A robot cannot occupy the same position as a ghost at the same time step.
- Robots and ghosts can cross each other (swap places) between time steps.
- S (start) is a neutral zone where collisions are ignored.

4. Door Activation
A door is open for the entire loop if any ghost will stand on its corresponding switch at any point during that loop. This follows time loop logic: future actions are predetermined. A robot cannot open a door for its own loop; only ghosts from previous loops can.

5. Victory Condition
The mission is complete when any robot reaches E.

6. Turn Counting
All loops run simultaneously, so the number of turns of a solution is defined as the length of a single padded output line. Padding with W counts as actual turns. W moves after reaching E still count towards the total number of turns.

7. Determining the Canonical Solution
If multiple solutions exist, the Canonical Solution is determined by applying the following criteria in order of priority:

7.1. Minimize Turns: Complete the mission in the fewest number of turns per loop. For example, a solution finishing in 7 turns per loop (with 2 loops) is preferred over one finishing in 9 turns (with 1 loop).

7.2. Minimize Loops: If the number of turns are equal, prefer the solution requiring fewer time loops (fewer output lines). For example, a solution with 2 loops (7 turns) is preferred over one with 3 loops (7 turns).

7.3. Lexicographical Order: If both the number of turns and the number of loops are equal, return the solution that is lexicographically largest. To compare two solutions, concatenate all their output lines into a single string (Loop 0, then Loop 1, etc.) and compare using standard string comparison. Since W has the highest ASCII value among the move characters, this ensures that robots prefer Waiting over performing unnecessary moves.
Input
Line 1: Three space-separated integers width height max_loops

Next height lines: The facility map (each row is width characters)
Output
N lines (where N ≤ max_loops), one per loop.

Each line contains space-separated single-letter moves: D L R U W (Down, Left, Right, Up, Wait).
Important: All paths must be padded to the same length using W.
Constraints
- 3 ≤ width ≤ 30
- 3 ≤ height ≤ 20
- 1 ≤ max_loops ≤ 5
- Every side of the grid is a wall (#).
- The grid contains exactly one S and one E.
- Each switch letter (a-z) and each door letter (A-Z) appears exactly once, forming a one-to-one correspondence.
- Every door is placed so that it blocks all possible routes through its location unless it is opened; it cannot be bypassed by walking around it.
- Every test case is guaranteed to have a solution.
Example
Input
5 6 2
#####
#a###
#S..#
###A#
#E..#
#####
Output
W W W W W U
R R D D L L
